title: aragallus oreophilus
intro: Aragallus oreophilus, also known as Western bugleweed, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce anxiety and insomnia due to its high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Aragallus oreophilus include its potential to lower blood pressure, act as an antispasmodic, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Aragallus oreophilus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, with herbalists often using it in conjunction with other herbs to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
